channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Warhammer AOS Daughters of Khaine Sylvaneth New Releases - Trees and Blood!
item.content:encoded:
This week sees a batch of new releases for two classic Warhammer Age of Sigmar armies: the tree-folk Sylvaneth and the bloodthirsty Daughters of Khaine. Games Workshop provided us with a sneak peek at new models and battletomes for each, so read on for our thoughts!

Daughters of Khaine Battletome

First up, I checked out the Daughters of Khaine Battletome, bringing the army into the latest edition of the game. Clocking in at around 100 pages, the tome starts with the high-quality lore we’ve come to expect from these.

We learn a bit about the army, their god Morathi-Khaine and how she came to dominate the Khainite cult, the importance of blood to the Daughters, and the various roles recognizable models play in the army - like the Slaughter Queens and Witch Aelves.

From there, we dive into the rules, and there are some fun new twists on the army. So much revolves around Anointed units. The Anointed Ritualist ability allows you to keep running abilities of Exalted units even after they’re destroyed. The Bloody Succession ability lets you name a new unit as Anointed. The Strident Warcry lets you give double charge actions to nearby units, and so on.

Additionally Blood Rites are triggered in numerous different ways, which give small buffs to your army. Then there are great Heroic Traits, like the Hand of Khaine which lets you attempt to auto-kill an enemy Hero. Add to that Artefacts of Power, Spells, Prayers, and Manifestations - and this aggressive army has everything it needs to truly be kitted out.

Now let’s check out two new units coming to the Daughters of Khaine army.

Daughters of Khaine - Blood Hags

The Blood Hags, apart from having a phenomenal name, serve as the battlefield elite for this army. Stats: Move: 6”, Save: 5+, Control: 1, Health: 1, Ward Save: 6+.

Their ranged weapon and melee weapons are fairly standard (though their melee weapon does have 3 attacks!), but they shine with their special abilities. The “Wicked Wardens” ability grants them Anti-charge (+1 Rend) whenever they’re near a Cauldron of Blood or Shrine of Dark Tribute (more on that below). And their “Crimson Offerings” special ability allows you to choose two blessings of Khaine instead of one whenever they destroy an enemy unit.

In other words, they want to stand near your shrines, chop up for foes, and get boosted up by celebrating their murder god.

Daughters of Khaine - Shrine of Dark Tribute

Also new to the army in pre-orders this week is the Shrine of Dark Tribute, a terrain feature guaranteed to help you spill some blood. Stats: Save: 4+, Health: 12, Ward Save: 6+.

Designed to look like a gorgeous, overflowing giant goblet of blood, the Shrine grant benefits to your army - so position it carefully! First the “Call to Murder” ability lets you switch up which Blessings of Khaine ability you’re working with, offering some adaptability during your Hero Phase.

Its other ability, “In Dark Communion,” lets you pick a friendly Daughters of Khaine Wizard or Priest within 3” of this terrain, allowing them to re-roll 1 casting roll or 1 chanting roll each turn. That’s… potentially game breaking if you build around it? Wow!

Now let’s get out of the blood and into the trees as we dive into the Sylvaneth.

Sylvaneth Battletome

Much like the Daughters Battletome, the Sylvaneth Battletome gives us great insight into the lore of this storied army. The lore brings us through the various realms of the Sylvaneth with gorgeous fold-outs, and the lore picks up on the story with Alarielle finally making her triumphant return to Ghyran and the forest of Athelwyrd.

There’s a lot of lore to work through here, and if you’re a fan of killer trees you’ll find a lot to love. But let’s highlight some of the cooler aspects to their updated rules. First, there’s their Battle Traits, which revolve around the idea of a creeping overgrowth taking over the battlefield. While a lot of this is generated by Awakened Wyldwoods, other unit abilities may also help you generate Creeping Overgrowth tokens. The more tokens, the more benefits you gain, like replacing lost units, giving units first strike, and shutting down your opponent’s commands.

There are also interesting battle traits, spells, etc. - but I really find it interesting how much of this army revolves around the creeping overgrowth. One of the battle traits, Realmroot Guide, allows you to move your hero from one area of the overgrowth to another during your movement phase - it’s almost like teleportation!

Prayers are also effected by the overgrowth, with the target of these prayers being either being visible to the priest or wholly within the creeping overgrowth. That’s kind of wild! There’s more to dive into, but we’ll keep it top-level for now as we check out the one new unit added to this army.

Sylvaneth - Grove Guardian

The Grove Guardian is a nurturing force on the battlefield, tending to her grove with a protective fury. Stats: Move: 4”, Save: 5+, Control: 2, Health: 7.

She attacks with her Grove Sickle or Shears (3 attack, 3+ hit, 4+ wound, Rend 1, D3 damage) and her powerful Guardian Sprites Companion (Crit (Mortal)) (6 attack, 4+ hit, 4+ wound, Rend 1, D3 damage). She’s a Priest (2), and has a potent special ability and unique prayer.

Her “Soulshriek” special ability depicts her shrieks of fury when her grove is harmed. Whenever a nearby model from a non-hero unit is slain while in combat, you roll a die and on a 5+ you inflict one mortal damage on that attacking enemy unit (after the fight ability has been resolved). I love the thematic feel of this, she could be 12” away from her flock but if she sees one getting hurt she shrieks so horribly it kills enemies. Very cool and evocative!

Next up her “Regenesis” prayer is very powerful. With a value of 4, you pick a friendly Sylvaneth unit wholly within the creeping overgrowth or within 12” of her, and make a D6 chanting roll. Roll a 4+ and you can return up to D3 slain infantry or dyads to that unit, otherwise heal (3) that target unit. If you roll a 6+ on your chanting roll, you can pick another eligible unit and do this again!

Daughters of Khaine and Sylvaneth New Releases - Final Thoughts

As with all of these new Battletomes, I really like the way that powers, abilities, and army-wide benefits are laid out, and I think both of these armies get some pretty nice upgrades. I don’t love that they’re sticking with the “your terrain that you have to buy from us is important” route with the Sylvaneth, as so much of their game now revolves around the Creeping Overgrowth ability that’s triggered mostly by having Awakened Wyldwood trees on the field.

But that’s how it’s always been for the Sylvaneth, and at least with this there’s a huge and growing benefit to playing trees in your list. As for Daughters of Khaine, I think this army is continuing to do what it does best - run fast at your enemies and try to chop them to bits.

What do you think of these two new updates to these classic armies? Let us know in the comments!

The Daughters of Khaine Sylvaneth New releases featured in this review were provided to us by Games Workshop.

Giaco Furino joined the TechRaptor team as a Staff Writer in 2019 after searching for a dedicated place to write and talk about Tabletop Games. In 2020, he… More about Giaco
